title: Cosmic Self Recognition
definition: The realization by life forms (e.g., humans, Buddha) that they are inherently connected and part of the unified, intelligent, conscious fabric of reality.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The idea that we, as living beings, are not separate from the universe but a part of it, capable of recognizing our interconnectedness with everything else.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The understanding that consciousness (e.g., in humans, Buddha) is an expression of the cosmic intelligence and not just an individual entity; it is a localized node within the unified fabric of reality.
difficulty_levels.expert: The insight that life forms (e.g., humans, Buddha) can achieve a fundamental realignment with the underlying, intelligent, conscious fabric of reality, becoming transparent conduits for universal knowledge and self-knowing.

extra_fields.insight: Life is not separate from the universe but an expression of it, capable of recognizing its own inherent interconnectedness.
